description: PS5 debugger payload - userland TCP wire-protocol server hosted inside SceShellCore (successor to GoldHEN/ps5debug)

changelog: **Added initial support for FW 8.x, 9.x, 10.x, 11.x, 12.x and 13.x.** ⚠️ These are *initial support - testing required* (not yet hardware-validated). Feedback from users on these firmwares is welcome.
**FW 6.x and 7.61 are hardware-validated.**
Previously these firmwares failed to install (`SceShellCore not found`) and, even when forced, showed **blank process names** and **truncated memory maps**. All fixed:
Install now succeeds.
`PROC_INFO` / `PROC_LIST` show correct process names.
`PROC_MAPS` returns the complete memory map.
**Firmware-agnostic process lookup** - the installer now locates the target via a stable system call instead of per-firmware kernel offsets, making install more robust across firmwares.
**Kernel-patch guard** - the installer now detects when the jailbreak has *already* applied the kernel patch and skips re-writing it, **preventing a kernel panic** seen on 7.61.
**Corrected per-firmware process-info offsets** - process name / path / title ID / content ID now read correctly on 6.x and newer (were frozen at 4.x values and came back empty on 6.x+).
Added **EchoStretch** and **Sonic-Iso** to the special thanks.
Features
CMD_PROC_MAPS now augments the vm_map list with Sony-aux regions
(SceGnm/SceAgc/JIT/DMEM) that bypass vm_map, discovered via a kernel-side
x86-64 page-table walk over the DMAP. FW-agnostic (runtime pmap-offset
discovery + ground-truth validation); falls back to the plain vm_map result
on any failure.
Hybrid memory-read path: reads route DMAP-direct for vm_map-absent (aux)
regions and mdbg otherwise (and as fallback), improve aux scans performance on
both the CMD_PROC_READ and server-side scan paths. No protocol change;
byte-identical results.
Hotfix: Fixed a rare save-corruption issue
Initial stable public release
